---
title: "4.4 Array Traversals"
subtitle: "Data Collections"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Array Traversals

### Traversing means visiting array elements systematically

This topic turns static storage into useful processing.

::: {.tight-list}
- <span class="term">traversing</span> an array means visiting each element or an ordered sequence of elements
- loops make traversal possible
- the index variable controls which element is being processed
:::

## Index Variables

### The index can be a variable and can appear inside expressions

![Reading array values with an index variable](../../assets/4-data-collections/4-4-array-traversals/arraywithindex.png){fig-align="center" width="74%"}

Students need to read expressions such as:

```java
arr[i]
arr[i - 1]
arr[i + 1]
```

This is the entry point for tracing traversal code accurately.

## Loops to Traverse Arrays

### Standard traversal starts at `0` and stops before `length`

![Indexed for-loop traversal](../../assets/4-data-collections/4-4-array-traversals/arrayForLoop.png){fig-align="center" width="78%"}

```java
for (int i = 0; i < arr.length; i++)
{
    System.out.println(arr[i]);
}
```

Both `for` loops and `while` loops can traverse arrays, but `for` is usually the clearest counted pattern.

## Arrays as Objects and Parameters

### Passing an array means passing a reference to the same array object

The textbook keeps this warning:

::: {.tight-list}
- arrays are objects in Java
- methods receive the array reference, not a copied array
- changing the array inside a method changes the original array
:::

This mirrors the reference behavior students saw in Unit 3.

## Partial Traversals and Common Errors

### Not every traversal uses the whole array, but every traversal must respect bounds

Key risks retained from the textbook:

::: {.tight-list}
- traversing only part of an array still needs a correct stopping condition
- using `<=` instead of `<` often creates an off-by-one error
- an invalid index causes <span class="term">ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException</span>
- returning from inside a loop too early can break a search algorithm
:::

## Enhanced For-Loop

### Use for-each when you need every value but not the index

![For-each comparison across notations](../../assets/4-data-collections/4-4-array-traversals/appinvForEachComparison.png){fig-align="center" width="76%"}

```java
for (int value : arr)
{
    System.out.println(value);
}
```

The enhanced `for` loop is concise and reduces index errors.

## For-Each Limitations and Arrays of Objects

### For-each reads values well, but it cannot replace every indexed traversal

::: {.tight-list}
- assigning a new value to the loop variable does <span class="mark">not</span> change the array element
- if the array stores primitive values, for-each cannot modify the stored values
- if the array stores object references, methods can modify the state of those objects
:::

So for-each is ideal for reading all values, but not always for editing array contents.

## Classroom Tasks

### Practice worth keeping

![SpellChecker challenge prompt](../../assets/4-data-collections/4-4-array-traversals/spellcheck.png){fig-align="center" width="44%"}

Retained classroom work for this topic:

::: {.tight-list}
- trace how index variables move through an array
- write full-array and partial-array traversals
- explain array-reference behavior in method calls
- identify off-by-one and early-return errors
- compare indexed loops with enhanced for-each loops
- <span class="term">4.4.9 Coding Challenge: SpellChecker</span>
:::

## Classroom Check

### A strong answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- define what an array traversal is
- write the standard loop condition for visiting all elements
- explain why `i < arr.length` is correct and `i <= arr.length` is not
- explain when an enhanced for-loop is appropriate
- explain why for-each cannot directly modify primitive values stored in the array
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
